content: 2025 state-of-the-art deep active learning memory agents can be redefined as recursively self-improving agents that treat their own existing agent modules as unlabeled data to actively sample and fine-tune, rather than treating only external environment data as the learning target. This recursive self-refinement loop leverages the active learning memory architecture to continuously prioritize which of its underlying agent components to update next, driven by intrinsic curiosity about its own performance gaps.
source_topics: active learning memory agent SOTA deep 2025
